explanation.summary: Carbs are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in C tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in C tier with an overall score of 21 from a base nutrition score of 21. Context barely changed the result.

topPros.title: very low downside whole food
topPros.explanation: Vegetables win a lot simply by offering clean nutritional payoff.

topPros.title: helps add volume without much calorie cost
topPros.explanation: That makes vegetables easier to use often.

topPros.title: good volume for the calories
topPros.explanation: That matters for satiety and general usefulness.

topCons.title: usually needs pairing to make a full meal
topCons.explanation: Most vegetables are not complete by themselves.

topCons.title: freshness and prep can matter
topCons.explanation: Practical friction is still a real downside.

topCons.title: taste and texture can limit compliance
topCons.explanation: A strong paper profile still needs real-world repeatability.

closing.summary: Mushrooms does have a few real-world upsides. but the nutrition case is still weak and the downsides drag it down hard. best only when you need a low-commitment add-on rather than a nutrient-dense anchor.
closing.useCaseNote: Best only when you need a low-commitment add-on rather than a nutrient-dense anchor.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

reviewChecklist: Verify nutrient values are sourced well enough for publishable use.
Review narration for category repetition and trim any awkward lines.
Confirm subtitle density stays readable at phone speed.
Replace placeholder hero/sprite art with final asset selection.
Check that the final tier verdict feels editorially defensible.
nextHumanStep: Review the generated narration + manifest, then use the manifest as the handoff into visual assembly / editing.
